message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "should quote exactly 3 alike runes as a block with 60 G base premium (plus first-insurance and fee)"
Expected: { premium: 71 } (60 base + 6 first-insurance + 5 fee = 71)
Current error: Expected 71, Received 88
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts

Note: 3 alike components qualify as a "block" with a special base premium of 60 G instead of 3×25=75. First insurance applies to the block base (10% of 60 = 6). Total: 60+6+5 = 71.

Implementation strategy: group items by type, detect groups of exactly 3 alike components (rune, moonstone), apply block pricing. Minimal: handle 3 alike components case.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the current implementation and make the minimal change to pass this test.
Now I'll implement the minimal change to detect a block of exactly 3 alike components.

content.text:
All 9 active tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Group items by type; when exactly 3 alike components (rune or moonstone) are detected, apply block pricing of 60 G base premium + 10% first-insurance instead of charging individually.
**Result**: All tests now pass (9 passing, 26 todo)
**Approach**: Minimal solution - hardcoded the 60 G block base and the specific component types ("rune"/"moonstone") that qualify for block pricing. Only the exact-3 case is handled; the "NOT block for 2 or 4" cases are future tests and will naturally pass through to individual pricing since the count check is strict equality to 3.
